keybinds.title: Hotkeys
app.title: MapArt Tools
app.subtitle: Minecraft map art schematic converter
metric.LAB.desc: Standard CIE76 metric in L*a*b* space with D65 white point. Good balance of speed and accuracy.
metric.CIEDE2000.desc: Improved CIEDE2000 metric with perceptual corrections. Most accurate for the majority of colors.
metric.RGB.desc: Simple Euclidean distance in RGB space. Fast, but ignores perceptual color differences.
metric.OKLAB.desc: OKLab color space (Björn Ottosson, 2020). Better hue linearity than CIE L*a*b*.
metric.WEIGHTED_RGB.desc: RGB weighted by eye sensitivity: red ×2, green ×4, blue ×3. Faster than LAB.
metric.LAB_D50.desc: CIE76 with D50 white point (ICC/print standard). More accurate for warm and neutral tones.
metric.CIEDE2000_D50.desc: CIEDE2000 with D50 white point. Maximum accuracy for print-industry colors.
metric.HCT.desc: Material Design 3 color space (CAM16 + L*). Best perceptual match for saturated colors.
metric.OKLAB_CHROMA.desc: OKLab with boosted chroma weight (×2). Better distinction of saturated colors.
metric.HSL.desc: Hue-Saturation-Lightness. Cylindrical RGB representation, intuitive for hue-based matching.
metric.HSV.desc: Hue-Saturation-Value. Similar to HSL but value-oriented, common in image editors.
metric.YUV.desc: BT.601 luma/chroma separation. Analog video standard, separates brightness from color.
metric.YCBCR.desc: BT.601 digital video standard (JPEG/MPEG). Separates luma from blue/red chroma.
metric.IPT.desc: IPT (Ebner & Fairchild, 1998). Perceptually uniform with good hue predictability.
metric.JZAZBZ.desc: JzAzBz (Safdar et al., 2017). Perceptually uniform with HDR support, outperforms OKLab for wide luminance range.
format.NBT.desc: Vanilla Minecraft structure format (.nbt). Compatible with the /place structure command.
format.LITEMATIC.desc: Litematica mod format (.litematic). Supports large structures with metadata.
format.SCHEM.desc: Sponge schematic format (.schem). Compatible with WorldEdit and Axiom.
format.MAP_DAT.desc: Minecraft map data format (.dat). Generates map_N.dat files ready to place in the saves/world/data/ folder.
btn.auto_fit_maps: Auto-fit: calculate map count to fit the image (⌈width/128⌉ × ⌈height/128⌉)
preview.cover_view: Fill grid completely (Cover, edges may be clipped)
preview.snap_tooltip: Snap image edges to grid edges while dragging
preview.one_map_view: Fit image to one map cell (1×1)

import.grid_label: Specify grid size (width × height in maps):
staircase.staircase.desc: Classic staircase: all three brightness levels (dark, normal, light). Maximum depth and detail.
staircase.valley.desc: Valley: staircase with height normalization. Structure goes down instead of up — easier to build.
staircase.dark.desc: Dark tones only. Flat map without steps.
staircase.light.desc: Light tones only. Flat map without steps.
staircase.flat.desc: Normal tones only. Completely flat map — simplest option for building.
staircase.flat_dark.desc: Flat map with dark tones. No steps, dark palette.
staircase.flat_light.desc: Flat map with light tones. No steps, light palette.
staircase.upwards_only.desc: Staircase upwards only: normal + light tones. Structure rises, never descends.
staircase.reverse_upwards_only.desc: Staircase downwards only: dark + normal tones. Structure descends, never rises.
algorithm.FLOYD_STEINBERG.desc: Classic error diffusion (1976). Spreads error to 4 neighboring pixels. Good balance of quality and speed.
algorithm.STUCKI.desc: Improved diffusion (1981). Spreads error to 12 pixels. Fewer artifacts than Floyd-Steinberg.
algorithm.JJN.desc: Jarvis-Judice-Ninke (1976). Error to 12 pixels across 3 rows. Soft result, great for photos.
algorithm.BURKES.desc: Simplified JJN (1988). Error to 7 pixels across 2 rows. Faster than JJN with similar quality.
algorithm.SIERRA3.desc: Sierra 3-row (1989). Error to 10 pixels. Soft, detailed result.
algorithm.SIERRA_LITE.desc: Simplified Sierra. Error to 3 pixels. Very fast, suitable for simple images.
algorithm.ATKINSON.desc: Bill Atkinson's algorithm (Apple, 1984). Spreads only 3/4 of error — preserves brightness, sharp edges.
algorithm.SIERRA2.desc: Sierra 2-row. Error to 7 pixels. Compromise between Sierra3 and Sierra Lite.
algorithm.FILTER_LITE.desc: Minimalist filter. Error to 2 pixels. Fastest diffusion algorithm.
algorithm.BAYER_2X2.desc: Bayer 2×2 matrix. Minimal ordered dithering. Visible pattern, very fast.
algorithm.BAYER_4X4.desc: Bayer 4×4 matrix. Classic ordered dithering. Great for pixel art.
algorithm.BAYER_8X8.desc: Bayer 8×8 matrix. Fine regular pattern. Standard for ordered dithering.
algorithm.BAYER_16X16.desc: Bayer 16×16 matrix. Very fine pattern, nearly invisible at distance.
algorithm.BAYER_3X3.desc: Bayer 3×3 matrix. Non-standard size — creates a less regular pattern.
algorithm.ORDERED_3X3.desc: Ordered 3×3 matrix (non-Bayer). Different threshold distribution — different pattern character.
algorithm.CLUSTERED_DOT.desc: Clustered dot 6×6. Simulates print halftone — dots group into clusters.
algorithm.HALFTONE.desc: Halftone screen 45° (8×8). Simulates newspaper print with angled dots.
algorithm.VOID_AND_CLUSTER.desc: Void-and-Cluster 8×8. Pseudo-random threshold distribution — minimal visible patterns.
algorithm.VOID_AND_CLUSTER_14X14.desc: Void-and-Cluster 14×14. Larger matrix — even fewer regular artifacts.
algorithm.FLOYD_STEINBERG_20.desc: Floyd-Steinberg with divisor /20 instead of /16. Slightly softer than original.
algorithm.FLOYD_STEINBERG_24.desc: Floyd-Steinberg with divisor /24. Even softer — fewer high-contrast transitions.
algorithm.FAN.desc: Fan algorithm. Single-row diffusion with fan distribution. Fast, minimal inter-row artifacts.
algorithm.SHIAU_FAN.desc: Shiau-Fan (1996). Improved Fan with an extra neighbor. Fewer streaks than original Fan.
algorithm.SHIAU_FAN_2.desc: Shiau-Fan 2. Modified weights variant. More uniform error distribution.
algorithm.PIGEON.desc: Pigeon. Single-row diffusion with symmetric back-distribution. Minimal inter-row artifacts.
algorithm.NAKANO.desc: Nakano. 3-row wide diffusion across 12 pixels. Smooth gradients, minimal banding.
algorithm.ZHOU_FANG.desc: Zhou-Fang. 2-row diffusion with diagonal emphasis. Sharp edges with soft transitions.
algorithm.DISPERSED_DOT_4X4.desc: Dispersed Dot 4×4. Maximum point separation — minimal visible clusters. Cleaner than Bayer.
algorithm.DISPERSED_DOT_8X8.desc: Dispersed Dot 8×8. Larger dispersed matrix. Even fewer visible clusters than 4×4.
algorithm.BAYER_32X32.desc: Bayer 32×32. Recursive extension of 16×16. Extremely fine pattern, nearly invisible.
algorithm.MAGIC_SQUARE_5X5.desc: Magic Square 5×5. Non-standard size based on a magic square. Unique non-recursive pattern.
algorithm.BLUE_NOISE_16X16.desc: Blue Noise 16×16. Suppresses low-frequency components. Organic look, minimal visible patterns.
algorithm.NONE.desc: No dithering. Each pixel is directly replaced by the nearest palette color.
